title: plesiatropha paniculata subsp. occidentalis
intro: Plesiatropha paniculata subsp. occidentalis, a subspecies of the Plesiatropha paniculata plant, is a medicinal plant native to tropical Africa, particularly in West and Central Africa. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant's health benefits include its ability to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antidote for snake bites. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, including infusions and decoctions, where it is often combined with other plants to enhance its medicinal properties.
